intro: ^It is the dawn of the Edo era.
^Tokugawa Ieyasu has crushed his last great rival at Sekigahara.
^His rule is new, and the land has not yet caught up with it —
^roving bandits and masterless samurai move through a country
^The warriors of Japan are learning a new game: not glory on
^the battlefield, but honor and standing under the Shogun.
^Many seek employment by proving themselves against the martial
^schools that dot the road between every village and city.
^You have two swords, the clothes on your back, and no master.
road_encounter: ^After days of uneventful travel you come to a narrowing in the road.
^Ahead, a man sits in the dirt with his back against a stone, eating cold rice.
^He watches you approach without turning his head.
^He is old. Unarmed. His sandal is broken, the strap hanging loose.

c-0: ^You walk past. He says nothing. His eyes follow you down the road.
c-1: ^You stop. He looks up at you without fear — the look of a man who
^"My sandal," he says simply, holding up the broken strap.

c-0: ^You crouch and retie the strap with cord from your pack. He
^"You have done this before," he says. "For someone else."
^You don't answer. You stand and walk on.
c-1: ^You look at the sandal. You look at him. You walk on.

c-2: ^He moves. He does not look at you again.

river_crossing: ^The road opens onto a shallow river crossing. The water is cold and
^clear. On the far bank, a boy is fishing.

b:
^As you wade across, the boy looks up.

^"The old man back there — that was my grandfather. He said a

^swordsman helped him."

^He holds out a small wrapped parcel. Rice and pickled plum.

^"He told me to wait."

^You take it. You wade across without another word.

^The boy glances at you and looks back at his line.

^You cross. The water is cold. The boy does not look up again.

^The boy sees you coming and gathers his things quickly, moving

^further down the bank.

^You cross alone. The river doesn't care what you did back there.

^Neither does the road ahead.

pass_warned: ^A merchant coming south stops when he sees you. He doesn't waste words.
^"Three men at the pass. Since morning. There's a woman."
^He goes. You have time to think.
^The road bends before it narrows. You leave it and move through the
^cedar line instead, coming up on the pass from the high side.
^From here you can see the shape of it before they see you.
pass_hostile_entry: ^Three men in the road. A woman with her back to the rock.
^They see you coming and the one doing the holding doesn't let go —
^"There he is," one of them says. "Swordsman who roughed up old Kenji
^The woman looks at you. She can't tell if you're help or more trouble.
^"You've got a reputation," the leader says. "Pay for it or bleed for it."

c-1: ^"Your purse," he says finally. "And we'll call it even."

c-0: ^You set your purse in the road between you.
^He picks it up without taking his eyes off you.
^They leave. The woman watches them go, then looks at you —
^trying to work out what she just saw.
c-1: ^He looks at you for a long time.
^Then he looks at the woman. Then back at you.
^Whether it was the words or something else, you'll never know.

pass_cold_entry: ^Three men in the road. A woman with her back to the rock, a hand
^at her collar. She is not fighting. She gave that up somewhere
^The man doing the holding is looking at you now.

b:
^You are above them. They haven't seen you yet.

^You could end this before it begins.

pass_scene: ^Watch. Wait for the shape of it.

b:
^You step into view already drawn.

^They spin toward you and find a sword at the distance of decision.

^The calculation takes no time at all.

^You don't speak. You draw and close the distance by half.

^He reads what he sees.

c-0: ^He releases the woman and steps back.
^"Not worth it," he says — to the others or to himself.
^They leave. The woman watches them go, then looks at you.
^You sheathe your sword. Nothing happened here.
c-1: ^He looks at you. He makes a calculation.
^One of the others moves to flank you. You turn toward him,
^and the leader takes that moment to swing.
c-2: ^The word lands wrong. He swings at the woman to show you

pass_fight: ^Go through them. Don't stop to count.

c-0: ^Two of them go down without dying. The third runs.
^The woman is free. The man who held her is sitting in the dirt,
c-1: ^You go through all three. More than you needed.
^The woman is free. The road is quiet in the way it goes quiet after.

pass_runner: ^One of them is already moving — scrambling up the embankment,

c-0: ^You watch him clear the ridge and disappear.
c-1: ^When you come back, the woman is gone.

pass_surrender: ^The man who held the woman is still on the ground. He looks up at you.
^He is not a warrior. Hunger has a particular look and he is wearing it.

c-0: ^You walk past. The woman is already gone.
c-1: ^You say it without looking at him.
^You hear him get to his feet and walk the other direction.

pass_aftermath_clean: ^The woman adjusts her collar. She looks at you the way people look
^when they don't know what to do with gratitude.
pass_aftermath_bloody: ^The woman is gone. You don't know when she left.
^The road is quiet. Your hands are steady.
^That's the part that stays with you.
pass_continues: ^The pass opens onto a wide valley. A line of cedars marks the road north.
^Dusk is coming. There is an inn at the base of the valley.
roadside_inn: ^The inn is small. A low table. A single lantern. Your swords
^close at hand, as they always are.

b:
^The serving girl comes to refill your cup. She leans close

^before she pours.

^"The men in the next room," she says quietly. "They've been

^asking about the swordsman traveling alone."

^She goes before you can answer.

roadside_inn: ^Through the paper wall, voices. Low. Unhurried.
^"How much do you think he's carrying?"
^You eat. You don't look at the wall.
^A long moment passes. Then the shadow.
^It moves across the paper slowly — the shape of a man bent low,
^trying not to make sound. It stops outside your door.
^A finger appears at the edge of the panel. It presses and rotates
^until a small dark circle opens in the paper.
^Set your bowl down. Reach for a fly.
inn_do_nothing: ^You eat. The eye watches. You give it nothing to read.
^A longer silence than before. Then the sound of them
^They decide it means you didn't notice.
^You wake to grey light and an empty room.
^Your pack has been opened. Your coin purse is gone.
^They left your swords — not out of mercy, you think.
^Out of fear of what morning would bring if they took them.
^You lie still for a moment looking at the ceiling.
inn_demonstration: ^You pause your meal without looking at the wall.
^Two flies have been circling the lantern. You watch them without
^appearing to watch them. Your chopsticks poised.
^Then you reach out and pluck them from the air.
^Clean. Unhurried. The way you might lift a cup.
^You set each down on the tatami by your knees, then go back
^The eye at the hole doesn't move for a long moment.
^Shuffling sounds. A low word you can't make out.
inn_speak: ^You stand. You don't draw. You face the wall.
^The eye disappears. A pause on the other side — the sound
^"Take what's on the table and go."
^Nothing else. Let the silence do the rest.

c-0: ^You set your coin purse on the table where the hole can see it.
^A moment. Then a hand comes under the gap at the bottom
^You sit back down. The rice has gone cold.
c-1: ^The paper wall is very thin. They know what is on
^this side of it and you know they know.

inn_preemptive: ^You draw in one motion and step through the paper wall.
^They were not ready for that. No one ever is.
^It is over quickly. More quickly than it needed to be.
^The innkeeper appears in the doorway. He looks at what's
^on the floor. He looks at you.
^You clean your sword. You leave coin on the table — more
^than the room cost. It doesn't cover what you did,
inn_morning: ^In the morning the inn is quiet.

b:
^You have no coin for the innkeeper. He looks at your face

^and waves you off without a word. He's seen this before.

^You step into the road feeling lighter than you should.

^Not free. Just empty.

^You leave a coin on the table. The serving girl is already

^up. She watches you go from the doorway without speaking.
^The road feels different this morning. Or you do.

inn_morning_early: ^You leave before dawn. The road is empty.
^Behind you, a light comes on in the inn.

b:
^The road north. No coin. Your swords.

^You have had less before. You try to remember when.

^The road north opens ahead of you, unhurried.

^Something settled in you last night.

^You're not sure when.

^The road north. Lighter by a purse.

^Peace costs something. It usually does.

^The road north. You walk quickly.

^You don't look back.

inn_departs: ^Somewhere ahead, above the treeline, the curved roof
^of a temple you have heard about.
hozoin_approach: ^The temple comes through the cedars before the road reaches it —
^a curved roof, the smell of incense, a bell that has already
^At the gate, a young monk watches you come up the path.
^He doesn't speak. He is deciding something about you.
hozoin_entry_compassionate: ^The young monk bows and leads you without being asked.
^Not to the practice yard. To a room overlooking the garden.
^An old man sits there with a cup of tea already poured for two.
^He is small and bent and has the eyes of someone who has been
^"I heard you helped an old man on the south road," he says.
^"And a woman at the mountain pass."
^He gestures to the cup across from him.
^"Sit. There is something I want to tell you before you meet my student."
hozoin_entry_middle: ^The young monk leads you to the practice yard without ceremony.
^An old man stands at the far edge of the courtyard.
^He does not introduce himself. He watches you arrive the way
^a man watches weather he has been expecting.
^At the center of the yard, a young monk is working through
^drills with a long spear. He moves the way water moves —
^no decision visible between one position and the next.
^He sets down the shorter spear and picks up the longer one.
hozoin_entry_cruel: ^The monk at the gate looks at your swords for a long moment
^He leads you to the practice yard quickly, like a man
^The yard is empty except for one figure.
^He is younger than you expected. Seventeen, perhaps.
^He is holding a spear as long as he is tall, and he is
^looking at you with an expression the monk clearly
hozoin_abbot_full_audience: ^The old man's name is In'ei. He was once the finest spear
^fighter in the province. Now he pours tea with hands that
^shake slightly, and he watches you the way he once watched
^"He was left at our gate in winter," he says. "An infant.
^We thought he would not survive the cold."
^He pauses. Not for effect. He is simply living in the memory
^"He took to the spear the way some children take to breathing.
^I cannot explain it. I have stopped trying."
^He turns his cup slowly in his hands.
^"He has beaten every monk in this temple. He is seventeen
^years old. He does not know what to do with that."

c-0: ^You listen. He takes this as understanding.
^"A boy like this — too much talent, too soon, too little
^of what softens a person. You know what I am describing."
^"If nothing humbles him, he will lead this temple into
^ruin. He will pick fights with men he cannot beat.
^He will believe his spear is proof of something it
^"I need someone to humble him. Not destroy him.
c-1: ^"Because you are the first person who has come up that
^road in a long time who might understand the difference
^between beating a man and breaking him."

hozoin_abbot_the_gamble: ^"I will not pretend this is without risk," he says.
^"He may hurt you badly. Or worse."
^He stands slowly, with the effort of a man who does this
^"He is the closest thing I have to a son."
^A long silence. The garden outside. A bird, somewhere.
^"I am making a very dangerous wager with something
^"Come. I will take you to him."
hozoin_fight_begins_with_knowledge: ^Inshun is already there. He has been waiting —
^not impatiently. He is incapable of impatience.
^He is simply present, the way a spear left
^He looks at you. He looks at In'ei.
^Something passes between them that you are not
^Then he turns back to you and raises the spear.
hozoin_fight_begins: ^His face is still becoming what it will be.
^But his hands on the spear are something else entirely —
^certain in a way that has nothing to do with age,
^as if they arrived in the world already knowing.
hozoin_the_fight: ^He is fast. Faster than anyone you have met.
^Not because he is trying to be fast. He is fast
^the way falling is fast — the most direct path,
^You have three exchanges before you understand
^that you are not fighting a student.

c-0: ^He reads it before you move. The spear finds
^You are good. He is something else.
c-1: ^He is not impatient. He will wait as long as you will,
^and he will be more rested at the end of it.
c-2: ^The spear is useless at close range.
^You close the distance in one step —
^— and find that he anticipated it.
^He reversed the spear before you finished the thought.
^The butt of it catches you across the chest.

hozoin_recognition_moment: ^Between one movement and the next, you see it.
^In his eyes. Something with no name —
^not rage, not hunger. Something older than both.
^Something that has been present since before
^You have felt this. You know where it comes from.

c-0: ^He does not press the advantage. He is waiting
^You look at him — not at the spear, at him —
^and you understand something you did not
^He fights because it is everything he has.
^He has known nothing else since the winter
^he was left on a temple step.
^You fight because you want to be the best.
^That is why you cannot beat him.

hozoin_after_recognition: ^He is watching you. Still. The spear is still raised
^but he is not attacking. He has felt something shift
^and he does not know what to do with it.

c-0: ^He stares at you. No one has done this before.
^No one at this temple can beat him, and no one
c-1: ^You raise your sword again — but something has changed
^You go three more exchanges. He beats you cleanly
^each time. On the third, he sweeps your feet and
^You look up at him from the dirt.
^He is looking back at you with an expression
^you have not seen on him before.

hozoin_blind_charge: ^He sees it coming the way he sees everything coming —
^You hit the ground. Hard. You don't get up immediately.
^When you do, the old man is there.
hozoin_surrender_outcome: ^Footsteps at the edge of the yard.
^The old man walks toward you slowly.
^He has been watching from somewhere you didn't see.
^"I was here the whole time," he says.
^He is not speaking to you. He is speaking to Inshun.
^Inshun says nothing. He is still watching you.
hozoin_defeated_with_grace: ^The old man comes from the edge of the yard.
^He says nothing for a moment. He looks at Inshun.
^"Up," he says finally. He extends a hand.
hozoin_abbot_appears_late: ^The voice comes from the edge of the yard.
^The old man walks toward you as if he was
^already on his way before you fell.
^"I was nearby," he says — in the tone of a man
^accounting for a coincidence that isn't one.
hozoin_abbot_speaks: ^He looks at Inshun for a long moment.
^Inshun looks back. There is something between them
^that has been accumulating for seventeen years.
hozoin_backstory_medium: ^"You saw something," the old man says.
^He glances at Inshun, then back at you.
^"He was left here as an infant. In winter. This spear
^is the only family he has ever known."
^"What you felt when you looked at him — hold onto it.
^Come back in fourteen days with it intact
^and you might actually teach him something."
hozoin_backstory_minimal: ^"He was left on these steps as an infant," he says quietly.
^"This spear is the only family he has known."
hozoin_the_kill: ^The spear is in his hands and then it isn't.
^After a long time, the old man comes from the edge
^of the yard. He walks slowly. He does not look at you.
^When he finally speaks, it is very quiet.
^"I knew this was possible," he says.
^He is not angry. He is sad in a way that seems
^He stays kneeling. He does not ask you to leave.
^The temple bell does not sound as you go.
hozoin_death: ^Inshun stands over you. He is breathing normally.
^The temple continues. The boy grows into the man
^In'ei needed him to become — humbled, eventually,
^by someone else, on some other day.
^The road you walked is still there.
hozoin_rematch_declared: ^Inshun has not moved. He is still watching you
^with that expression you cannot read yet.

^"And I just wagered his life on a stranger

hozoin_the_woods: ^The forest is tall. The light comes through in long columns.
^Behind you, the temple. Somewhere inside it, a boy
^with a spear who is also thinking about fourteen days.
^You don't know yet what you're training for.
^That is not quite true. You know exactly what
^You don't know yet who you're trying to become.
hozoin_exit_no_rematch: ^The forest is there. You walk into it.
